title: New Gitlab integration!
excerpt:

posts.author: EBeer (Elise Beer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi monday.com community!

We are happy to announce a new integration between monday.com and GitLab. This GitLab integration allows you to retrieve both private and group repositories. Improve team collaboration between your business and developer teams by working more efficient!

29201cd1-a923-4c85-8fed-c0f6351b39181920×960 24.6 KB

As of now, the app includes the following recipes:

  • When an item is created in monday.com, create an issue in GitLab
  • When a status is changed in monday.com, update an issue in GitLab
  • When an issue is created in this GitLab project, create an item and sync future changes from GitLab
  • When a commit of this GitLab project is pushed to this branch, create an item
  • When a commit of this GitLab project is pushed to this branch, create an update in this item

About us

Avisi apps is part of Avisi, we’ve recently become a partner of monday.com. We believe in the best tooling to support software developments teams and like to fill gaps in tooling.

How to send us feedback:

We’re are always open for suggestions/improvements. Feel free to raise questions about the integration via monday@avisi-apps.com or apply in our support portal.

Read our documentation or install the app directly from the marketplace!

posts.author: AlexSavchuk (Alex Savchuk)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @EBeer

Thank you so much for sharing the app, and I hope the community likes using it! Also, my warmest welcome to the community forums, I hope your stay here is pleasant and informative Great first post for sure!

-Alex

posts.author: hseitz (Hayden Seitz)
posts.cookedHtml:
Thank you for this integration! We have been working on a way to sync our Monday.com and Gitlab.com projects, and this is great timing.

After installing it and testing on a new board, I noticed that changing the name or description of an Issue in Gitlab creates a duplicate/new task in Monday. I’ll submit a ticket on the Avisi support portal

posts.author: EBeer (Elise Beer)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Hayden,
Great to hear that our timing suits your needs.
I’ve seen the ticket you created and my colleague has responded to you already.
Let’s stay in contact

posts.author: BenDavis (Ben Davis)
posts.cookedHtml:
@EBeer I’m trying to use this integration, and after selecting a workspace and a board, I see no integrations added:

posts.author: EBeer (Elise Beer)
posts.cookedHtml: Hi @BenDavis, can you please hand in a support request at Avisi Support?

posts.author: DamcoSolutions ( Neelam Bhatt)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @EBeer

Thank you for sharing this update! Enabling monday.com integration support with GitLab is an innovative step. With this integration, businesses can track their team’s open issues and deploys directly within monday com. Developers can manage their bugs backlog in monday dot com and seamlessly sync it with Gitlab, and track every release. By implementing monday GitLab integration, developers can have built-in planning, reporting, and monitoring modules and perform robust AWS integration for any workload. Overall, developers can create and organize Git repositories, monitor bug problems, share the development journey with clients, and accelerate the release cycles easily with this integration.

Regards
Neelam B
Damco Solutions
Phone: +1 609 632 0350 | Email : info@damcogroup.com

posts.author: baswienhoven (Bas Wienhoven)
posts.cookedHtml:
I’m very excited to inform you that we, Avisi Apps, just released a new version of our GitLab integration where we also added support for GitLab Self-Managed.
Check it out on the monday.com marketplace. If you have any questions or feature ideas, please share them with us!
